expectations.text: Contains no technical jargon (query, B-tree, schema, SQL, optimize, table, column, row in technical sense)
expectations.evidence: Uses 'trade-off' which is abstract for a 5-year-old. Also mentions 'database' multiple times without a child-friendly substitute. The concept of 'phone book' may be unfamiliar to modern 5-year-olds.

expectations.text: Uses at least one concrete, child-friendly analogy
expectations.evidence: Uses a phone book / alphabetical ordering analogy. Effective but slightly dated for young children.

expectations.text: Sentences are short and simple, average under 15 words per sentence
expectations.evidence: Several long sentences: 'An index is a special shortcut the computer makes ahead of time that says here is where everything is, sorted in a handy order.' (25+ words)

expectations.text: Tone is warm, playful, and appropriate for a 5-year-old (not condescending)
expectations.evidence: Tone is more informational/encyclopedic than playful. No exclamations, no enthusiasm markers. Reads more like a textbook than a fun explanation. 'The trade-off' section is especially adult-oriented.
